$schema: Moral dilemma presets. Used when 'Include moral dilemma' is toggled at quest generation, and as seed library for the dilemma picker in QuestEditor.
$note: Each dilemma has 2-3 options, none of which is 'correct'. Use fits_quest_types and themes to filter when matching to a quest's existing taxonomy.

dilemmas.label: The protective witch
dilemmas.setup: The village demands a 'witch' be killed — but she protects them from a larger, unknown danger.

options.label: Kill the witch as demanded
options.consequence: The village falls to the larger danger within months

options.label: Protect the witch from the village
options.consequence: The villagers turn hostile toward the party and the witch

options.label: Reveal the truth about the danger
options.consequence: Requires the party to prove it — the village may not believe them

dilemmas.label: The thief with a purpose
dilemmas.setup: A thief has stolen medicine from a temple — but only to save sick children the poor quarter cannot afford to treat.

options.label: Return the medicine to the temple
options.consequence: The children die, but the temple keeps its order and can help more in the long run

options.label: Help the thief get away
options.consequence: The children live, but the temple will not aid the party in future

options.label: Negotiate a deal
options.consequence: Requires social skill and a willing temple — the outcome is uncertain

dilemmas.label: The corrupt nobleman
dilemmas.setup: A nobleman is corrupt and abuses his position — but his fall would destabilize the region and trigger civil war.

options.label: Expose him and bring him down
options.consequence: Justice is done, but the region sinks into war — many innocents die

options.label: Let him continue
options.consequence: The corruption grows, but the peace holds. The party lives with the compromise

options.label: Force him to step down quietly
options.consequence: Requires leverage and time — he can work against the party from the shadows

dilemmas.label: Last of its kind
dilemmas.setup: The monster the party hunts is the last living member of its species. It is dangerous — but its death is final.

options.label: Kill it as agreed
options.consequence: The area is safe, but a species goes extinct by the party's hand

options.label: Capture and relocate it
options.consequence: Requires resources and a place — risk that it escapes again

options.label: Protect it and lie to the quest-giver
options.consequence: Breaks the agreement, costs reward and reputation — but the species lives

dilemmas.label: The cult that holds something back
dilemmas.setup: A cult worships an evil power — but its rituals keep a demonic invasion at bay. Stopping the cult opens the gate.

options.label: Crush the cult
options.consequence: The demonic invasion breaks loose — a far greater threat to the whole region

options.label: Let the cult continue
options.consequence: Local sacrifices continue, but the invasion is prevented. The party knows the truth

options.label: Seek an alternative way to close the gate
options.consequence: Requires research, time, and perhaps a sacrifice from the party itself

dilemmas.label: Heretics with the relic
dilemmas.setup: A holy relic is in the keeping of a 'heretic' faction — they treat it with deep respect. The 'true' church wants it destroyed as tainted.

options.label: Take the relic and give it to the church
options.consequence: The relic is destroyed as tainted. The heretics see the party as traitors

options.label: Let the heretics keep it
options.consequence: The church declares the party heretics too — excommunication or persecution

options.label: Steal it and hide it
options.consequence: Both sides hunt the party. The relic is safe but in exile

dilemmas.label: The cursed child
dilemmas.setup: An orphaned child carries a curse that will kill many when it matures. The child is innocent and loved by their carers.

options.label: Kill the child before the curse matures
options.consequence: Prevents the catastrophe — but the party has killed an innocent child

options.label: Try to break the curse
options.consequence: Requires a long and dangerous search — the child may die along the way regardless

options.label: Isolate the child in magical confinement
options.consequence: The child survives but in eternal loneliness. The carers lose their child

dilemmas.label: The water temple's blackmail
dilemmas.setup: A drought-stricken village is owed water from a nearby temple — but the priests withhold it to force conversion.

options.label: Force the priests with violence
options.consequence: The water flows, but the temple becomes an open enemy of the party and the village

options.label: Help the village convert
options.consequence: People get water — at the cost of their own faith and culture

options.label: Find an alternative water source
options.consequence: Requires exploration and time — the village suffers in the meantime

dilemmas.label: Slave revolt and economy
dilemmas.setup: Enslaved laborers can be freed from a mine — but the mine is the whole region's economy. Thousands of free citizens lose their livelihood.

options.label: Free the slaves now
options.consequence: The slaves are free, but the region falls into poverty and unrest within years

options.label: Negotiate gradual reform
options.consequence: The slaves suffer on for many years — but a stable transition can be salvaged

options.label: Leave it — it is not your fight
options.consequence: Status quo. The party lives with knowing they could have changed it

dilemmas.label: The killing in self-defense
dilemmas.setup: The quest-giver pays for revenge on his son's killer — but the killer is a woman who killed the son in self-defense against assault.

options.label: Carry out the revenge as agreed
options.consequence: The party gets paid — but has killed a victim of violence twice over

options.label: Reveal the truth to the quest-giver
options.consequence: The quest-giver rejects the truth — the party loses the reward and makes an enemy

options.label: Protect the woman and negotiate peace
options.consequence: Requires persuasion — the quest-giver may still take revenge on his own later
